coverage.framework_detected: Spring Boot 2.7.0 (Java 11): Web, Security, Data JPA, Validation, H2

api_design.detail: GET endpoints return ResponseEntity with explicit status; POST /uploadMood returns void with no status/body contract, making client error handling and API documentation harder.
uploadMood mixes implicit Mood binding with separate @RequestParam fields; this is unconventional and easy to misuse compared to a single JSON DTO or consistent form parameters.
Small demo surface with clear verb-style paths; acceptable for a prototype though not fully RESTful.

domain_structure.detail: Clear separation of controller, service, repository, model, and config packages.
Repository package is spelled repositary, which hurts discoverability and consistency.
Mood entity doubles as API payload and persistence model; a DTO would reduce coupling and clarify API contracts.

business_logic.detail: getBestLocationFromCoordinate uses latitude in both terms of the Euclidean distance (second term should use longitude), producing incorrect nearest-location results.
For each happy coordinate, iterates all locations loaded from DB; acceptable for tiny datasets but does not scale and loads full location set repeatedly.
MoodServiceImplementation delegates persistence to repository and keeps orchestration in one place.

data_access.detail: JPQL queries for distribution, happy coordinates, and locations are explicit and parameterized with :remoteUser.

data_access.check: Entity ID type vs repository generic
data_access.detail: Mood declares int id with @GeneratedValue but MoodRepository extends CrudRepository<Mood, String>; ID types should align to avoid subtle persistence bugs.
MoodDistribution and Coordinate as Spring Data projections are appropriate for read-only query results.

authentication.detail: HTTP Basic with in-memory users and hardcoded passwords is acceptable only for local demos; production would need HTTPS, external identity, and secret management.
CSRF is disabled globally for the API while H2 console gets targeted CSRF ignore; state-changing POST endpoints are unprotected from CSRF in browser contexts.
All application routes require ROLE_USER; person field is set from authenticated remote user on upload.
SpringSecurityConfig extends WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter, deprecated in favor of SecurityFilterChain bean configuration in newer Spring Security.

validation.detail: uploadMood does not annotate Mood with @Valid; only some @RequestParams use @Validated, so entity field constraints may not run consistently on the mood object.
Latitude/longitude clamped to [-10,10] is arbitrary for real geography; fine for demo but misleading if treated as real coordinates.
MoodStatus enum carries @NotBlank on an internal field; this does not meaningfully validate incoming enum request values.

error_handling.detail: No @ControllerAdvice or Problem Details; clients receive default Spring error payloads without consistent application-level errors.
uploadMood returns void and does not set response status explicitly; persistence failures surface as generic servlet errors without domain context.
getBestLocationFromCoordinate can return null when no locations exist; callers may need explicit handling.

gaps.detail: getBestLocationFromCoordinate uses latitude in both terms of the Euclidean distance (second term should use longitude), producing incorrect nearest-location results.

gaps.check: Entity ID type vs repository generic
gaps.detail: Mood declares int id with @GeneratedValue but MoodRepository extends CrudRepository<Mood, String>; ID types should align to avoid subtle persistence bugs.
